product_truth: Stratum has completed Phases 1 through 8 of the migration plus Phase C deployment readiness hardening. The product-facing system remains the stored report workflow only: create a report run, track real run status, open stored published reports, and access real artifact availability. The deployment contract now separates web and worker runtimes, blocks report creation when the worker dependency is not healthy, and exposes an honest minimal readiness surface.

chosen_stack.auth: Auth.js with Google OIDC and JWT sessions

critical_rules: Do not invent work beyond the approved handoff scope
No live ATS or AI calls on report read paths
Keep the stored-report architecture as the only product-facing path
forbidden_scope: Do not re-open product-model invention work
Do not refactor unrelated UI for polish
known_risks: Auth foundation was built but not browser-verified end-to-end in the recorded implementation sessions
One unrelated lint warning remains in src/components/ui/ServiceInterruptionModal.tsx
A hosted Postgres vendor such as Neon was not actually established in the approved source truth
The web runtime can verify worker heartbeat freshness, but it still does not inspect worker backlog depth or throughput
